compliance_matrix.title: Institutional Integrity
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Systemic Candor Breakdown. Redacting statutory SACSCOC checkpoints from public announcements; burying the April 2026 Academic Referral Report; utilizing local network proxies to block external tracking of internal records.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Scrub detailed dockets; execute a multi-semester blanket agenda scheme to project an illusion of total administrative control.

compliance_matrix.title: Institutional Mission & Review
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Mission Subversion. Unilaterally abandoning the traditional liberal arts HBCU mandate to force a compressed polytechnic layout without a faculty-led, 5-year cyclical mission evaluation.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Package radical institutional transformations under a generic 'Academic Plan' heading to bypass explicit public mission modification approvals.

compliance_matrix.title: Continuous Operation
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Operational Interruption. Slashing core degree programs unilaterally under Senate Bill 185 before SACSCOC evaluates or authorizes teach-out frameworks, resulting in severe student class-action breach of contract lawsuits.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Mask rapid programmatic shutdowns under standard committee headings to dodge immediate teach-out and enrollment scrutiny.

compliance_matrix.title: Fiduciary Oversight & Distinction
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Fiduciary Abandonment. Fast-tracking a 'Proposed Farm Purchase' on the June 11 Finance docket despite an active -$59M unrestricted deficit; bypassing open floor debate on 100% compliance failure audits.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Bury the devastating CLA Single Audit inside a non-debatable Consent Agenda; approve macro capital expansions via unitemized blanket votes.

compliance_matrix.title: Multi-Level Governance
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Oversight Failure. Bypassing Council on Postsecondary Education (CPE) operational boundaries while failing to clearly define local policy and authority lines on the public record.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Hide explicit regulatory restrictions, state fiscal oversight mandates, and takeover mechanics from public-facing board records.

compliance_matrix.title: Control of Intercollegiate Athletics
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Athletic Budget Siphoning. Executing an unmonitored $29.5M internal pool raid to cover baseline payroll; siphoning academic and tuition dollars to mask massive athletic overruns.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Omit cost-versus-income athletic ledger comparisons from public files; route asset trace lines behind a closed executive session firewall.

compliance_matrix.title: Control of Fund-Raising Activities
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Fund-Raising Oversight Exposure. Failure of the Chief Executive Officer to maintain distinct, uncorrupted boundaries over internal donation paths; co-opting advancement networks to mask an operational cash bleed.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Combine advancement and finance dockets under generic committee headings; hide unitemized donor allocations in closed session lines.

compliance_matrix.title: Institution-Related Entities
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Oversight Breach. Failing to maintain independent, written legal authority, formal agreements, and strict liability definitions regarding third-party asset management.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Omit structural partnership details; fast-track capital agreements via unitemized, blanket committee votes.

compliance_matrix.title: Qualified Academic Officers
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Repeat Qualification Failure. Operating with an active Dean of Arts & Sciences who completely lacks a doctoral degree or the mandatory 5 years of department-chair level administrative experience.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Route executive qualification reviews and personnel actions directly into a closed executive session under KRS 61.810.

compliance_matrix.title: Personnel Appointment & Evaluation
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Arbitrary Layoffs. Weaponizing statutory exigencies to execute arbitrary non-faculty support staff layoffs; bypassing mandatory performance evaluation protocols; replacing internal oversight personnel with external contractors.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Omit organizational restructuring charts; bundle mid-level staff liquidations under single-line administrative adjustments to avoid open floor debate.

compliance_matrix.title: Faculty Adequacy & Sufficiency
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Hollowed Faculty Chassis. Slicing academic lines to float baseline operations; refusing to provide program-specific faculty metrics to SACSCOC review teams to hide instructional gaps.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Enforce a permanent public index freeze on Faculty Senate minutes; leave dead committee tracking links active on the web directory.

compliance_matrix.title: Faculty Development
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Resource Starvation. Systematically defunding instructional support, professional training, and research development pools to channel remaining cash into central operations.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Mask line-item operational resource depletion under generic 'Faculty Workload' and campus restructuring titles.

compliance_matrix.title: Institutional Planning
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Planning Collapse. Executing a top-down academic reorganization completely detached from systematic, data-driven institutional research and assessment.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Bypass institutional effectiveness data; push sweeping academic reductions under standard committee dockets.

compliance_matrix.title: Student Achievement
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Evaluation Blackout. Failing to identify, evaluate, or publish verified student success thresholds and graduation benchmarks to SACSCOC reviewers.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Hide explicit curriculum rejections noted in the hidden April Referral Report; scrub student outcome data from public view.

compliance_matrix.title: Program Content, Length, & GenEd
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Academic Liquidation. Unilaterally compressing program content, shortening degree lengths, and gutting the general education core below the mandatory 30-hour federal threshold.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Conceal curriculum gaps behind vague headings; avoid open-floor debates on individual credit-hour reductions.

compliance_matrix.title: Archived Information
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Document Spoliation. Wiping or breaking historical public catalogs and degree tracks to obscure the scale of academic cuts executed under SB 185.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Break digital archive links; let network proxies obscure historical degree tracking structures to stop students from verifying breached contracts.

compliance_matrix.title: Academic Governance
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Shared Governance Void. Extinguishing faculty legislative authority over the curriculum; using an unconstitutional 'Ghost Senate' to rubber-stamp top-down cuts.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Terminate public minute indexing; block faculty whistleblowers from flagging accelerating financial aid diversions.

compliance_matrix.title: Admissions Policies
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Recruiter Blindspot. Operating without transparent, published admissions policies or independent contractor recruitment oversight.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Hide the exact contractual and fiscal linkages connecting admissions pipelines to outsourced, underwater digital frameworks.

compliance_matrix.title: Evaluating Transfer Credit
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Credit Manipulation. Awarding and transcribing external transfer or competency-based credits without verified faculty-led validation.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Fast-track third-party credit transcription parameters behind closed executive session doors.

compliance_matrix.title: Cooperative Academic Arrangements
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Unvetted Outsourcing. Transcribing student credits through third-party arrangements (Magellan Framework) with zero faculty senate evaluation or quality vetting.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Bundle multi-million dollar vendor renewals under vague, single-line actions; hide partnership evaluations from the open public floor.

compliance_matrix.title: Library & Information Resources
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Starved Learning Infrastructure. Diverting designated student library and technology fees into central treasury cash flows; allowing core database and electronic journal subscriptions to expire.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Mask severe library resource depletion and expired database subscriptions under generic 'Faculty Workload' and restructuring headings.

compliance_matrix.title: Student Support Services & Complaints
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Support Starvation. Hollowing out student services personnel and hoarding student affairs complaint logs to hide systematic contract breaches from reviewers.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: House student complaints in disconnected administrative offices; keep staff directories unindexed on the public site.

compliance_matrix.title: Financial Resources & Physical Assets
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Operational Cash Collapse. Operating on a negative 280-day operational cash runway; forcing reliance on unmonitored internal inter-fund raids just to float baseline operating payroll.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Table actual property remediation and acquisition costs to private retreats; mask fiscal freefall behind unitemized consent votes.

compliance_matrix.title: Policy Compliance Statement
compliance_matrix.vulnerability: Policy Defiance. Willfully executing academic cuts and structural reorganizations in direct violation of the Commission's Substantive Change and Public Disclosure policy statements.
compliance_matrix.firewall_strategy: Hide explicit policy requirements; frame major academic alterations as internal administrative procedures detached from external oversight.
